task.title: GPU Status Check
task.description: Monitor GPU health and performance metrics to ensure system stability.

solution.approach: Analyze available tools and commands to monitor GPU status. Prioritize CLI commands like `nvidia-smi` for real-time GPU metrics including utilization, memory usage, temperature, and power draw. Check if NVIDIA drivers and CUDA are properly installed.
solution.algorithm: Check command availability: `which nvidia-smi`. If available, parse output for key metrics: GPU name, utilization %, memory used/total, temperature (C), power usage (W).

solution.approach: Design a monitoring workflow that executes `nvidia-smi` with appropriate flags to extract health indicators: --query-gpu=index,name,utilization.gpu,memory.used,memory.total,temperature.gpu,power.draw --format=csv,noheader,nounits. Ensure output is human-readable and suitable for logging or alerting.
solution.algorithm: Use `nvidia-smi` with structured query format to get consistent, parsable output without headers or units for easy parsing.
solution.complexity: O(n) where n is number of GPUs

solution.approach: Execute the designed `nvidia-smi` command using `run_shell_command` to capture live GPU metrics. Do not redirect output to files; return stdout directly for immediate inspection.

Validate the output by checking for non-empty response and ensuring all expected fields are present. Confirm GPU is responsive and metrics are within normal operating ranges (e.g., temperature < 85°C, memory usage reasonable). If no output or errors, flag as degraded state.
solution.algorithm: Parse CSV output line-by-line; validate each field is numeric where expected; compare against thresholds; generate summary status.

solution.approach: Analyze available tools and commands to monitor GPU status. Prioritize CLI commands like `nvidia-smi` for real-time GPU metrics including utilization, memory usage, temperature, and power draw. Check if NVIDIA drivers and CUDA are properly installed.
solution.algorithm: Check command availability: `which nvidia-smi`. If available, parse output for key metrics: GPU name, utilization %, memory used/total, temperature (C), power usage (W).
